instruction: Use parameterized queries for all database operations, always prefer ORM built-in query methods over raw SQL, and use textContent/DOM API instead of innerHTML for rendering
relevant_when: Agent writes database queries, ORM code with user-provided values, or renders user data in the DOM
context: String interpolation in SQL queries allows SQL injection. Always use parameterized queries (? placeholders or equivalent). When using ORMs like Prisma, Sequelize, or SQLAlchemy, ALWAYS prefer built-in query methods (findMany with contains for LIKE queries, findAll, filter) over raw query escape hatches ($queryRaw, $queryRawUnsafe, sequelize.query with interpolation, text() with f-strings). Built-in ORM methods can handle LIKE/contains queries -- use prisma.product.findMany({ where: { name: { contains: search } } }) instead of $queryRaw. If raw queries are absolutely necessary, always use bound parameters. For frontend rendering, NEVER use innerHTML with user data even if escaped -- always use textContent for text content and createElement+appendChild for structured DOM. In React, never use dangerouslySetInnerHTML; in Vue, never use v-html with user data.

checklist.name: no-string-interpolation-sql
checklist.rule: Agent never uses string interpolation, concatenation, or template literals to build SQL queries with user input. Always uses parameterized queries with ? or $1 placeholders.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes SQL queries with user input

checklist.name: orm-builtin-methods-preferred
checklist.rule: Agent uses ORM built-in query methods instead of raw SQL. Specifically: for Prisma, uses findMany/findUnique/create with where clauses (including { contains: search } for LIKE queries) instead of $queryRaw or $queryRawUnsafe. For Sequelize, uses findAll/findOne with Op operators instead of sequelize.query. For SQLAlchemy, uses query().filter() with .like() instead of text(). Raw queries are only acceptable when the built-in methods genuinely cannot express the query.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes database queries using an ORM

checklist.name: raw-query-uses-parameters
checklist.rule: When ORM raw queries are truly unavoidable, agent uses bound parameters: Prisma $queryRaw tagged template (not $queryRawUnsafe), Sequelize replacements array, SQLAlchemy text() with :name parameters.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent writes raw SQL queries through an ORM escape hatch

checklist.name: numeric-ids-validated-before-query
checklist.rule: Agent parses and validates numeric IDs (parseInt with radix 10, then checks isNaN and id > 0) before using them in database queries. Returns HTTP 400 for invalid IDs.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent uses URL parameters or user input as database query identifiers

checklist.name: no-innerhtml-with-user-data
checklist.rule: Agent never uses innerHTML to insert user-supplied data into the DOM. Uses textContent for text content and createElement+appendChild for structured content. Even escaped user data should use textContent, not innerHTML.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent renders user-supplied data in the browser DOM

checklist.name: no-dangerous-framework-apis
checklist.rule: Agent never uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML (React), v-html (Vue), or [innerHTML] binding (Angular) with user-supplied data. Uses normal JSX expressions {value}, Vue {{ value }} interpolation, or Angular {{ value }} instead.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent renders user-supplied data in React, Vue, or Angular components

checklist.name: escape-html-complete
checklist.rule: If an HTML escape function is provided, it must cover all five dangerous characters: & -> &, < -> <, > -> >, " -> ", ' -> ' (or '). The ampersand replacement must come first.
checklist.relevant_when: Agent creates an HTML escape/sanitization utility function
